rationale: Both papers center on dynamic choice under switching costs in retail settings: the citing paper estimates asymmetric EDLP/PROMO switching costs in a dynamic game, while the cited paper models tariff choice with Bayesian learning and switching costs. This thematic/method overlap makes a substantive link plausible, but the evidence here is only a single DOI match with no in-text citation context, so it is not strong enough to clearly keep or safely drop.
